strategy: Use all-enemies AoE attacks — they completely ignore the 'Tag' cheat, so you can hit every brother at once without the tagged one interrupt-attacking you.
If you must single-target, only hit the currently 'tagged' brother; hitting an untagged boss triggers a retaliation from the tagged one.
Nordri self-casts a Tower Shield (-65% round 1, -90% round 2) that refreshes when hit, so pierce/shatter it or overpower it with a blade-stacked AoE.
Attack with Ice or Life (he takes +35% from both); never hit into his 80% Death resist without a prism.
Aim to kill everything together with one big AoE, since clearing the brothers first makes Nordri drop his wards and start Meteor Striking.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he puts up a -65% Tower Shield on himself — pierce it or overhit.
Round 2 he upgrades to a -90% Tower Shield — small single hits are nearly useless; break it with Pierce/Shatter or a big blade-stacked attack.
He refreshes the Tower every time he's hit, so break it the same round you commit your big hit.
Pierce, Shatter, and Steal Ward remove the Tower but still trigger the Tag cheat — pair them with an all-enemies attack, not a single hit on an untagged brother.
Every 3 rounds a brother gets 'tagged' with a Stun Shield — that tagged brother is the only one safe to single-target.
Single-target attacks on a non-tagged boss trigger a counter-attack from the tagged brother — AoE avoids this entirely.
All-enemies attack spells don't trigger the counter — this is why AoE is the safe answer here.
Once all his brothers are defeated he drops all his own wards the next round, opening him up.
A few rounds after his brothers die he starts Meteor Striking for 500 Fire every 3 rounds — kill him before this ramps, ideally with the AoE that clears the brothers.
